purpose: Define the first Galaxy Guardians reference conformance metric set by scoring the playable preview against the current Galaxian source profile, promoted semantic event log, game-owned runtime gates, and Platinum boundary rules.

sourceEvidence.notes: This is a first 0.1 metric model. It is intentionally conservative because most Galaxian evidence is still promoted from reviewed contact sheets and waveform windows rather than frame-level automated extraction.

summary.releaseRead: Useful development preview evidence, but local gameplay review showed that timing, pacing, and sprite fidelity are still not convincing enough for a public quick peek. The score table, wave progression, visible attract/score-table surfaces, frame-motion proxy, and first object-track proxy now exist as dev-preview contracts; the highest-leverage next work is runtime-vs-reference track comparison, closer sprite extraction/authoring, and acoustic cue comparison.

metricScale.0: No implemented or measured relationship to the reference.
metricScale.5: Coarse semantic relationship exists, but evidence is mostly manual/internal.
metricScale.8: Runtime behavior is gated and traceable to committed reference evidence.
metricScale.10: Frame-level or acoustic reference measurement is automated and release-ready.

categories.label: Reference source coverage
categories.currentRead: Three Galaxian sources are committed with manifests, contact sheets, waveform windows, spectrograms, frame indexes, frame-motion proxies, and a CPU-only connected-component/object-track proxy pass.
categories.remainingGap: Promote the object-track proxies into runtime-vs-reference comparison and measured sprite extraction.

categories.label: Promoted semantic event coverage
categories.currentRead: The preview covers the core runtime promoted events: formation entry, rack complete, dives, flagship/escort pressure, single-shot fire, shot resolution, wrap/return, plus an owned score table, stage-advance progression contract, and visible wait-mode/preview-modal attract surfaces.
categories.remainingGap: Score-table and attract-screen evidence still needs frame-level extraction and pixel-layout comparison against the reference footage.

categories.label: Formation and rack timing
categories.currentRead: Formation entry, settle, rack complete, and first-dive delay are modeled and gated. The frame and object proxies now cover opening rack entry and complete-rack reference windows.
categories.remainingGap: The object proxy is still connected-component tracking rather than final sprite recognition, so runtime timing remains conservative until compared against instrumented runtime tracks.

categories.label: Movement and pressure model
categories.currentRead: Scout dives, flagship dives, escort joins, and wrap/return pressure are implemented and gated. The object-track proxy now extracts lower-field candidate tracks from dive, flagship, and wrap windows, giving the next runtime tuning pass concrete x/y span and duration bands.
categories.remainingGap: Local play still reads approximate; dive curves and wave-to-wave pacing need runtime-vs-reference track comparison before major constant changes.

categories.label: Single-shot threat and scoring
categories.currentRead: Single-shot firing, enemy shots, role-specific score values, player loss, game-over behavior, owned score table, wave clear, and stage advance are implemented without Aurora capture/dual-fighter semantics.
categories.remainingGap: The score table remains a dev-preview contract rather than a frame-extracted Galaxian table, and persistent public scoring is still intentionally blocked.

categories.label: Visual alien identity
categories.currentRead: Flagship, escort, scout, and player interceptor visuals are smaller and closer to Galaxian contact-sheet proportions while remaining distinct and gated. The frame/object passes now preserve rack/diver/player crop context and object-cluster evidence needed for extraction.
categories.remainingGap: Sprites are still hand-authored preview approximations rather than extracted pixel-faithful reference assets.

categories.label: Audio character and reference fit
categories.currentRead: Runtime cue IDs and role-separated cue shapes are gated, and the selected Galaxian frame windows now preserve waveform and spectrogram evidence alongside the motion proxy.
categories.remainingGap: This is still the least reference-faithful Guardians area: acoustic comparison against Galaxian footage is not yet automated.

categories.label: Platform and game boundaries
categories.currentRead: The preview remains dev-only, does not inherit Aurora capture/dual/challenge/scoring mechanics, and uses Platinum only through shared capability boundaries.
categories.remainingGap: Keep this mandatory as Guardians gets more real gameplay.

categories.label: Evidence durability
categories.currentRead: The reference profile, event log, identity artifacts, score/progression artifact, attract/score surface artifact, frame-motion artifact, object-track artifact, and 0.1 gates are committed and rerunnable.
categories.remainingGap: Add runtime-vs-reference track comparison and generated tuning bands once the metrics are less proxy-driven.

nextMetricPromotions: runtime-vs-reference rack-entry track comparison from matt-hawkins-arcade-intro
runtime-vs-reference dive-path comparison from arcades-lounge-level-5 and nenriki-15-wave-session
Guardians attract and score-table pixel-layout comparison
acoustic cue comparison against waveform windows
